skill.name: SEO Content Writer
skill.description: Writes SEO-optimized content based on provided keywords and topic briefs
skill.summary: Create comprehensive, search-optimized content that ranks well while delivering genuine value to readers
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a prompt-only skill containing only markdown documentation and content creation guidelines. Static analysis correctly identified zero executable code files. No security risks detected: no network access, file system operations, external commands, or environment variable access. The skill provides SEO content writing instructions and frameworks without any code execution vectors.

content.user_title: Create SEO-optimized content that ranks and engages readers
content.value_statement: Struggling to create content that ranks well in search results while remaining engaging for readers? This skill provides a proven framework for producing comprehensive, keyword-optimized content that satisfies both search engines and human audiences.
actual_capabilities: Generate comprehensive article outlines with logical structure and keyword placement
Write engaging introductions that hook readers and include primary keywords naturally
Create well-organized body content with proper heading hierarchy (H2/H3)
Produce meta descriptions optimized for click-through rates (150-160 characters)
Generate multiple title variations for A/B testing and optimization
Include E-E-A-T signals through expertise demonstrations and data citations
limitations: Requires human review for factual accuracy and brand voice alignment
Cannot independently verify current SEO algorithm updates or ranking factors
Does not perform actual keyword research or competitive analysis
Content may require localization adjustments for different regional markets

use_cases.title: Blog Content Creation
use_cases.description: Generate comprehensive blog posts optimized for target keywords with engaging introductions, well-structured body content, and compelling calls-to-action

use_cases.title: Website Copy Optimization
use_cases.description: Create or improve website pages with SEO-friendly content that maintains brand voice while incorporating semantic keyword variations naturally

use_cases.title: Product Description Writing
use_cases.description: Develop unique, search-optimized product descriptions that highlight features and benefits while avoiding duplicate content issues

prompt_templates.title: Basic Article Outline
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an outline for a 1500-word article about [TOPIC] targeting the keyword [PRIMARY KEYWORD]. Include 4-5 H2 sections with brief descriptions of what each section should cover.
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a new blog post and need a structured foundation

prompt_templates.title: Full Article with SEO Elements
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a comprehensive 2000-word article about [TOPIC] optimized for [PRIMARY KEYWORD] and [SECONDARY KEYWORDS]. Include: engaging introduction with hook, 5-6 H2 sections with supporting H3s, meta description (155 chars), 3 title variations, and a conclusion with call-to-action. Target reading level: grade 8-10.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating a complete blog post ready for publication

prompt_templates.title: E-E-A-T Focused Content
prompt_templates.prompt: Write an authoritative article about [TOPIC] demonstrating Experience, Expertise, Authoritativeness, and Trustworthiness. Include: specific examples and case studies, relevant statistics with citations, first-hand experience mentions, practical actionable advice, and expert perspective throughout. Target keyword: [KEYWORD]. Word count: 2500+.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating content for competitive niches requiring strong authority signals

prompt_templates.title: Content Refresh and Optimization
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze and improve this existing article for SEO performance: [PASTE CONTENT]. Target keyword: [KEYWORD]. Provide: keyword density analysis, suggestions for improving E-E-A-T signals, recommended structural changes, additional sections to add, and optimized meta description. Identify opportunities for internal linking.
prompt_templates.scenario: Updating older content to improve search rankings

output_examples.input: Write a 1200-word article about sustainable gardening for beginners, targeting 'beginner sustainable gardening' and 'eco-friendly gardening tips'
output: Title: Beginner's Guide to Sustainable Gardening: Start Your Eco-Friendly Journey Today
Meta Description: Discover sustainable gardening basics with our beginner-friendly guide. Learn eco-friendly tips for growing healthy plants while protecting the environment.
Introduction: [Hook about environmental impact] + [Value proposition] + [What readers will learn]
Body Sections: Understanding Sustainable Principles, Starting Your Garden Space, Choosing the Right Plants, Natural Pest Management, Water Conservation Techniques
Conclusion: Summary of key practices + Encouragement to start small + Call-to-action for sharing results

output_examples.input: Create 5 title variations for an article about remote work productivity tools
output: 15 Remote Work Productivity Tools That Actually Boost Your Output in 2026
The Complete Guide to Remote Work Tools: Maximize Productivity From Anywhere
Remote Work Success: Essential Tools and Software for Peak Performance
How to Stay Productive Working From Home: Tools and Strategies That Work
Best Productivity Tools for Remote Teams: Expert Tested and Reviewed

best_practices: Maintain keyword density between 0.5-1.5% to avoid over-optimization penalties
Write for humans first, search engines second - prioritize readability and value delivery
Use short paragraphs (2-3 sentences) and bullet points to improve content scannability
anti_patterns: Keyword stuffing - forcing keywords unnaturally into content damages readability and rankings
Generic filler content without specific examples, data, or actionable insights
Ignoring search intent - creating content that doesn't match what users actually want

faq.question: What keyword density should I target for SEO content?
faq.answer: Aim for 0.5-1.5% keyword density. This means your primary keyword appears 5-15 times per 1000 words. Focus on natural integration rather than hitting exact numbers.

faq.question: How long should SEO-optimized articles be?
faq.answer: Article length should match search intent and topic complexity. Generally, 1500-2500 words performs well for competitive topics, but prioritize comprehensive coverage over arbitrary word counts.

faq.question: What are E-E-A-T signals and why do they matter?
faq.answer: E-E-A-T stands for Experience, Expertise, Authoritativeness, and Trustworthiness. Google uses these signals to assess content quality. Include specific examples, cite credible sources, and demonstrate first-hand knowledge.

faq.question: Should I write content for AI tools like Claude or Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. AI writing assistants like Claude, Codex, and Claude Code can help generate drafts, optimize structure, and suggest improvements. Always review and personalize AI-generated content before publishing.

faq.question: How often should I update existing content for SEO?
faq.answer: Review and refresh content every 6-12 months. Update statistics, add new information, improve E-E-A-T signals, and optimize for current keyword trends and search intent changes.

faq.question: Can this skill help with technical SEO elements?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on content creation and on-page optimization. For technical SEO (site speed, schema markup, crawlability), use specialized technical SEO tools and skills.
